channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: The Air Canada Crash: Before and After the Frantic Call to ‘Stop, Stop, Stop’
item.description: How a cascade of seemingly minor events led to the worst runway disaster at LaGuardia Airport in decades.
item.dc:creator: John Leland, Pranav Baskar, Olivia Bensimon, Ashley Southall, Corey Kilgannon and Jan Ransom
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

media:content.media:credit: Shannon Stapleton/Reuters

item.title: ‘A Young Woman Opened the Door on the Other Side and Slid in Next to Me’
item.description: Sharing a cab with the catch of the day, where two friends met and more reader tales of New York City in this week’s Metropolitan Diary.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 29, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Ultimátums descabellados y bombardeos ‘a mansalva’: un retrato de Trump en guerra
item.description: Es evidente que los drásticos vaivenes del presidente Trump se han combinado para dar a su gestión de la guerra un carácter errático e improvisado.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Los aliados del presidente siempre han dicho que su imprevisibilidad es su superpoder, y que eso mantiene a sus enemigos en vilo.

item.title: Los últimos días de Gregory Bovino: sin arrepentimientos y con mano dura
item.description: El rostro de la ofensiva migratoria de Trump se retira esta semana y afirma que no fue lo suficientemente lejos en sus acciones.
item.dc:creator: Katie J.M. Baker and Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:credit: Kathleen Flynn para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Bovino en diciembre con agentes de la Patrulla Fronteriza en Nueva Orleans

item.title: ‘BTS: El regreso’: 5 puntos clave
item.description: El documental de Netflix sobre la exitosa banda de k-pop ofrece una visión íntima de los músicos mientras lidian con su legado e intentan trazar la siguiente fase de sus carreras.
item.dc:creator: Regina Kim

media:content.media:credit: Netflix, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: From left, the BTS members Jin, Suga, Jimin, V, Jung Kook and RM in a scene from the documentary “BTS: The Return.”

item.title: I Saw Something New in San Francisco
item.description: Marshall McLuhan was right about Claude, too.
item.dc:creator: Ezra Klein

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Whitaker for The New York Times

item.title: ¿Has visto los ‘pucheros de la generación Z’?
item.description: Tienen los labios fruncidos. Sus ojos miran al vacío. ¿Es este gesto una forma de identidad?
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann

media:content.media:credit: Pascal Le Segretain/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: El “puchero de la Generación Z” se ha atribuido a varias jóvenes famosas, entre ellas Lily-Rose Depp, quien parece fruncir los labios con precisión forense cuando posa para las fotos.

item.title: When Sports Heroes Have Terrible Opinions
item.description: Sports used to be an oasis from political squabbling. But now the bad opinions of star athletes have become impossible to escape.
item.dc:creator: Devin Gordon

item.title: Goodbye, ‘Queer Eye.’ Goodbye, Queer Acceptance.
item.description: Today even tolerance is in doubt.
item.dc:creator: Rosa Rankin-Gee

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Tehran Hit by Heavy Airstrikes as U.S. Marines Arrive in Middle East
item.description: Airstrikes pummel residential areas and a university in Iran’s capital. Houthi forces in Yemen entered the widening conflict with a missile attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times

item.title: Quote of the Day: On Iran’s Rugged Frontier, Kurds Yearn to Join the Fight
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, March 29, 2026.

item.title: A Toothless Iran? Missile and Drone Strikes Show It Can Still Inflict Pain.
item.description: A wave of strikes across the Middle East in recent days shows that Iran has not lost the capacity to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish, Helene Cooper, Isabel Kershner and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Emergency responders at the site of a strike in the town of Eshtaol, Israel, on Saturday.

item.title: For These Running Influencers, No Van or Bathtub Is Too Small for a 5K
item.description: For these influencers, there’s no space too small for a jog.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cohen

item.title: The Slum in Gangnam, the Richest Part of Seoul
item.description: The city wants to redevelop a shantytown in Gangnam district, where hundreds are defying eviction, fighting for a right to own a home in an area notorious for the exorbitant cost of housing.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun and Chang W. Lee

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times

item.title: Why Marriage, for So Many, Is Less Appealing Than Ever
item.description: From Gen Z to Gen X, a pause in the march to the altar, or a decision to skip it altogether, is becoming more common.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: Corrections: March 29, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, March 29, 2026.

item.title: Record Number of T.S.A. Employees Called Out on Friday
item.description: President Trump signed a memo late Friday ordering the Department of Homeland Security to restore pay to airport screeners.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel, Sonia A. Rao and JoAnna Daemmrich

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Security lines at LaGuardia Airport in New York City on Friday.

item.title: ‘No Kings’ Protesters Rally From Coast to Coast
item.description: More than 3,000 demonstrations were planned in the United States on Saturday as part of the “No Kings” movement. Protesters held signs condemning the war in Iran and immigration raids.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times

item.title: Astronaut’s Condition That Led to Space Station Evacuation Remains a Mystery
item.description: The astronaut, Michael Fincke, experienced a medical emergency in January that rendered him unable to speak, he said.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:credit: NASA/Bill Ingalls , via, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Mike Fincke, a NASA astronaut, said doctors aren’t sure what caused his medical episode, which prompted an evacuation from the International Space Station.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: The war with Iran escalated on Saturday as the Iran-allied Houthis joined the conflict and Israel bombarded Tehran with airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Emergency responders at the site of a strike in Eshtaol, Israel, on Saturday.

item.title: Roundabouts
item.description: Michael and Oliver Schlossberg’s puzzle will drive you crazy.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Mary F. Calvert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Merge!

item.title: Vance Wins CPAC Straw Poll, but Rubio Gains Steam Among MAGA Faithful
item.description: Conference organizers presented the poll results as a rebuttal to narratives that Republicans were split over the Iran war and support for Israel.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Elizabeth Frantz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance won the CPAC straw poll for the second consecutive year, but his margin of victory was narrower this time.

item.title: Henry C. Lee Dies at 87; Forensic Scientist Testified in Defense of O.J. Simpson
item.description: The Times called him “the world’s most highly regarded forensic criminologist,” but later in his career he faced accusations that he had hidden and fabricated evidence.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman

media:content.media:credit: Jessica Hill/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Henry C. Lee in 2011 at the University of New Haven in Connecticut, where he was a professor for more than 50 years. He had many high-profile cases, but none drew more attention than the 1995 murder trial of O.J. Simpson.

item.title: G.O.P. Rift Leaves Congress With No Clear Path to End the Shutdown
item.description: The deadlock that left the Department of Homeland Security shuttered highlighted Republican divisions that are flaring ahead of the midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Department of Homeland Security remained shut amid a bitter fight among House and Senate Republicans.

item.title: Dr. Judith L. Rapoport, Pioneer in Understanding O.C.D., Dies at 92
item.description: Her best-selling 1989 book, “The Boy Who Couldn’t Stop Washing,” based on her groundbreaking research, brought public awareness to obsessive-compulsive disorder.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel
item.category: Boy Who Couldn't Stop Washing, The (Book)

item.title: F.B.I. Said to Dig Up Old Investigative Files on Democratic Lawmaker
item.description: The urgent instructions at the Trump administration’s behest to gather and relay the files on Representative Eric Swalwell have alarmed some career law enforcement officials.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong and Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Eric Swalwell of California, one of President Trump’s most vocal critics in the House, said that he was being targeted by the Trump administration.

item.title: ‘No Kings’ Protests Decry Trump and His Agenda
item.description: Thousands of organized demonstrations stretched across the country. Minnesota was a focal point of the protests after a tumultuous immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times

item.title: James Tolkan, a Tough-Talking Actor in ‘Back to the Future’ and ‘Top Gun,’ Dies at 94
item.description: Mr. Tolkan’s career spanned decades but his breakout roles came as an authority figure in two popular films of the mid-1980s.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz

media:content.media:credit: CBS, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: As Cmdr. Tom “Stinger” Jardian in “Top Gun,” Mr. Tolkan gives a verbal lashing to young pilots who lacked discipline.

item.title: 10 Million Grill Brushes Recalled After Some People Ingested Loose Bristles
item.description: In at least five cases, customers sought medical attention to remove bristles “from their digestive tract or throat,” a federal agency said.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:credit: Colin Clark for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Nexgrill said consumers should stop using the recalled brushes as the potential ingestion of bristles could lead to “serious internal injuries that could require surgery.”

item.title: Ukraine Finalizes Air Defense Deals With Gulf Nations Amid War in Mideast
item.description: The Ukrainian president visited the Middle East, where he sought to shift his country from a recipient of military aid to a supplier.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ukrainian Presidential Press Service, via Agence France-Presse
media:content.media:description: In a photo released by the Ukrainian Presidential Press Service, President Volodymyr Zelensky, in black, and Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad al-Thani of Qatar, center left, walk together during their meeting in Doha, Qatar, on Saturday.

item.title: Man in Paris Accused of Attempted Attack on Bank of America
item.description: Prosecutors say the police thwarted an attacker as he was trying to detonate explosives. The episode is being investigated as possible terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler

media:content.media:credit: Sebastien Dupuy/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Police officials outside a Bank of America site in Paris on Saturday.

item.title: Los herederos de Castro emergen en medio de la crisis en Cuba
item.description: Mientras los funcionarios de Trump exigen cambios, la familia Castro reaparece en la escena política de Cuba. Algunos incluso se preguntan: ¿Podría alguno de ellos ser la “Delcy de Cuba”?
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero and David C. Adams

media:content.media:credit: Mauricio Lima para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Honrando a Fidel Castro en Santiago de Cuba tras su muerte en 2016.

item.title: Israeli Strike Kills 3 Journalists in Southern Lebanon, Officials Say
item.description: Israel accused one of the reporters of being a Hezbollah operative. Lebanon’s president said they were journalists and condemned the killings.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Mohammed Zaatari/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A police officer checks a charred car in the town of Jezzine in southern Lebanon on Saturday, after an Israeli strike that killed three Lebanese journalists.

item.title: Stephen Miller’s Latest Low
item.description: His project of subordination at home and abroad continues unabated.
item.dc:creator: Jamelle Bouie

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times

item.title: No Kings Protests Held Across the U.S.: Photos and Videos
item.description: It’s the third time that the coalition behind the “No Kings” movement has organized events to protest President Trump and his policies. In the United States, more than 3,000 demonstrations were planned.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño and Sonia A. Rao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Protesters gather in front of the Idaho State Capitol during the No Kings Day protest in Boise, Idaho.

item.title: A Robust Caesar for Broccoli Lovers
item.description: Trusty white beans and roasted broccoli get the garlicky, assertively umami Caesar treatment.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Vanni for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Spencer Richards.
media:content.media:description: Kristina Felix’s broccoli and white bean Caesar.

item.title: Everything With Trump’s Signature, Name and Likeness: Currency, Buildings and More
item.description: Since the start of his second term, there have been more than a dozen instances of his name, image or signature emblazoned on a variety of American initiatives and institutions.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Cai

item.title: April Monthly Bake: Carrot Cake Butter Mochi
item.description: The spice of carrot cake meets the springy and buttery texture of mochi cake, with a tangy cream cheese glaze for good measure.
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.
media:content.media:description: Vaughn Vreeland’s carrot cake butter mochi.

item.title: 4 consejos para prosperar (a pesar de las dificultades)
item.description: Las rutinas y rituales diarios pueden ayudarte a disfrutar los buenos momentos y a sobrellevar los malos.
item.dc:creator: Jancee Dunn

item.title: Pakistan Set to Host Top Regional Diplomats for Talks on Iran War
item.description: The foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Egypt are expected in Islamabad on Sunday as the Pakistani government strives to find a diplomatic solution to the war.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan at the United Nations in 2024. His country has recently intensified efforts at shuttle diplomacy between the United States and Iran.

item.title: The Biggest Names in Fine Dining Now Have Rock-Star Residencies
item.description: From Las Vegas to the Côte d’Azur, high-profile chefs are taking their cuisine on the road.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Kronsberg

media:content.media:credit: Frank Wonho Lee
media:content.media:description: For a residency of the Chicago restaurant Alinea at the Maybourne Beverly Hills last summer, remodeling the space alone cost “close to $400,000,” according to the restaurant’s top chef.

item.title: Present Company
item.description: Entertaining out-of-town visitors can yield ideas for how to live more intentionally in your everyday life.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: The Old Man Dreaming Up Wars for Young Men to Fight
item.description: Any deal with Iran would probably be a bad one. But sending ground troops to seize Iranian territory would be an even worse choice.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kristof
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Mark Peterson for The New York Times

item.title: ‘We Are Going to Deeply Regret This War’
item.description: Three journalists on the potential repercussions of Operation Epic Fury.
item.dc:creator: Robert Siegel, E.J. Dionne Jr., Mona Charen and Jillian Weinberger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; photograph by Marcos del Mazo/Getty

item.title: Trump Does Anything He Wants — and More
item.description: When you’re a wannabe king they let you do it.
item.dc:creator: Maureen Dowd
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Roberto Schmidt/Getty Images

item.title: Iran Thinks It Can Win a Long War
item.description: A costly war will make Iran’s enemies think twice before attacking again.
item.dc:creator: Dina Esfandiary and Ziad Daoud
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: ‘Project Hail Mary’ Is Fun. Maybe That’s All It Takes.
item.description: There’s been a vibe shift in Hollywood. Fun movies are back.
item.dc:creator: Roy Price

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; source images by Film Audio Services, Encyclopaedia Britannica Films, Silverwell Films and Archive Films/Getty Images

item.title: U.S. Marines Arrive in Middle East, as Houthis Enter War
item.description: A U.S. official said that earlier an Iranian strike at a military base in Saudi Arabia injured around two dozen U.S. troops, one of the most serious breaches of American defenses since the war began.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
